title: Disagreeing About Workload
summary: Teammates Lisa and James have an honest conversation about an uneven workload on their project and try to find a fair solution.

body.speaker: Lisa
body.text: James, do you have a moment? There's something I'd like to talk about.

body.speaker: Lisa
body.text: I've been feeling a bit overwhelmed lately. I think the workload between us isn't very balanced right now.

body.speaker: James
body.text: Oh, I didn't realise that. Can you tell me more about what you mean?

body.speaker: Lisa
body.text: Well, I've been handling all the client emails, preparing the weekly reports, and managing the project timeline.

body.speaker: James
body.text: I see. I wasn't fully aware of how much you were doing. I'm sorry about that.

body.speaker: Lisa
body.text: I'm not saying it to blame you. I just think we need to divide the tasks more fairly.

body.speaker: James
body.text: You're right. What would you suggest?

body.speaker: Lisa
body.text: Maybe you could take over the weekly reports starting next week.

body.speaker: James
body.text: I can do that. The reports usually take about two hours, right?

body.speaker: Lisa
body.text: Yes, around two hours. That would free up a lot of time for me.

body.speaker: James
body.text: What about the client emails? Those seem to take up a lot of your time too.

body.speaker: Lisa
body.text: If you want, we could share that responsibility. You handle the emails on Monday and Wednesday, and I'll do Tuesday and Thursday.

body.speaker: James
body.text: That sounds very reasonable to me. I'm glad you brought this up.

body.speaker: Lisa
body.text: I was a little nervous to say something, but I think it's important to be honest with each other.

body.speaker: James
body.text: Absolutely. Good communication makes the whole team work better.

body.speaker: Lisa
body.text: Let's also check in with each other every Friday to make sure the balance stays fair.

body.speaker: James
body.text: Great idea. I'm happy we talked about this openly.

sentences.speaker: Lisa
sentences.text: James, do you have a moment? There's something I'd like to talk about.
sentences.translationVi: James, bạn có lúc nào rảnh không? Có điều gì đó tôi muốn nói chuyện.

sentences.speaker: James
sentences.translationVi: Tất nhiên. Mọi thứ có ổn không?

sentences.speaker: Lisa
sentences.text: I've been feeling a bit overwhelmed lately. I think the workload between us isn't very balanced right now.
sentences.translationVi: Tôi cảm thấy hơi quá tải gần đây. Tôi nghĩ khối lượng công việc giữa chúng ta không được cân bằng lắm lúc này.

sentences.speaker: James
sentences.text: Oh, I didn't realise that. Can you tell me more about what you mean?
sentences.translationVi: Ôi, tôi không nhận ra điều đó. Bạn có thể nói rõ hơn ý bạn muốn nói là gì không?

sentences.speaker: Lisa
sentences.text: Well, I've been handling all the client emails, preparing the weekly reports, and managing the project timeline.
sentences.translationVi: Vâng, tôi đã xử lý tất cả email của khách hàng, chuẩn bị báo cáo hàng tuần và quản lý tiến độ dự án.

sentences.speaker: James
sentences.text: I see. I wasn't fully aware of how much you were doing. I'm sorry about that.
sentences.translationVi: Tôi hiểu. Tôi không hoàn toàn nhận thức được bạn đang làm bao nhiêu công việc. Tôi xin lỗi về điều đó.

sentences.speaker: Lisa
sentences.text: I'm not saying it to blame you. I just think we need to divide the tasks more fairly.
sentences.translationVi: Tôi không nói điều này để đổ lỗi cho bạn. Tôi chỉ nghĩ chúng ta cần phân chia công việc công bằng hơn.

sentences.speaker: James
sentences.text: You're right. What would you suggest?
sentences.translationVi: Bạn nói đúng. Bạn đề xuất gì?

sentences.speaker: Lisa
sentences.text: Maybe you could take over the weekly reports starting next week.
sentences.translationVi: Có lẽ bạn có thể nhận phụ trách báo cáo hàng tuần bắt đầu từ tuần sau.

sentences.speaker: James
sentences.text: I can do that. The reports usually take about two hours, right?
sentences.translationVi: Tôi có thể làm điều đó. Báo cáo thường mất khoảng hai tiếng, đúng không?

sentences.speaker: Lisa
sentences.text: Yes, around two hours. That would free up a lot of time for me.
sentences.translationVi: Đúng, khoảng hai tiếng. Điều đó sẽ giải phóng rất nhiều thời gian cho tôi.

sentences.speaker: James
sentences.text: What about the client emails? Those seem to take up a lot of your time too.
sentences.translationVi: Còn email của khách hàng thì sao? Có vẻ như chúng cũng chiếm nhiều thời gian của bạn.

sentences.speaker: Lisa
sentences.text: If you want, we could share that responsibility. You handle the emails on Monday and Wednesday, and I'll do Tuesday and Thursday.
sentences.translationVi: Nếu bạn muốn, chúng ta có thể chia sẻ trách nhiệm đó. Bạn xử lý email vào thứ Hai và thứ Tư, còn tôi sẽ làm vào thứ Ba và thứ Năm.

sentences.speaker: James
sentences.text: That sounds very reasonable to me. I'm glad you brought this up.
sentences.translationVi: Điều đó nghe rất hợp lý với tôi. Tôi vui vì bạn đã đề cập đến điều này.

sentences.speaker: Lisa
sentences.text: I was a little nervous to say something, but I think it's important to be honest with each other.
sentences.translationVi: Tôi hơi lo ngại khi nói điều gì đó, nhưng tôi nghĩ điều quan trọng là phải thành thật với nhau.

sentences.speaker: James
sentences.text: Absolutely. Good communication makes the whole team work better.
sentences.translationVi: Hoàn toàn đúng. Giao tiếp tốt giúp cả nhóm làm việc hiệu quả hơn.

sentences.speaker: Lisa
sentences.text: Let's also check in with each other every Friday to make sure the balance stays fair.
sentences.translationVi: Hãy cũng kiểm tra với nhau mỗi thứ Sáu để đảm bảo sự cân bằng vẫn công bằng.

sentences.speaker: James
sentences.text: Great idea. I'm happy we talked about this openly.
sentences.translationVi: Ý kiến hay. Tôi vui vì chúng ta đã nói chuyện cởi mở về điều này.

hintVocab.meaningVi: kiểm tra tình hình với nhau

grammarNotes.title: Present perfect for recent situations (I've been feeling…)
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Thì hiện tại hoàn thành tiếp diễn 'I've been + V-ing' diễn tả một hành động hay trạng thái đã kéo dài từ quá khứ đến hiện tại. Ví dụ: 'I've been feeling overwhelmed lately' — cảm giác này đã kéo dài một thời gian gần đây.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: 'I've been + V-ing' (present perfect continuous) describes a situation that started in the past and continues now. E.g. 'I've been feeling overwhelmed lately' — the feeling has lasted for a recent period.

grammarNotes.title: Modal 'could' for suggestions
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'could' để đưa ra gợi ý mềm mỏng, ví dụ: 'Maybe you could take over the weekly reports.' 'Could' ít áp đặt hơn 'should'.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'could' to make a polite suggestion, e.g. 'Maybe you could take over the weekly reports.' 'Could' is less forceful than 'should'.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why is it sometimes difficult to tell a colleague or manager that you feel the workload is unfair, and what can you do to make that conversation easier?
